summary: Russian forces dropped FAB-250 bombs on Kramatorsk causing injuries and damage to multiple buildings, and on Druzkovka killing a 70‑year‑old pensioner. Ukraine reported several UAV incursions across northern and central regions, while Russian officials announced plans for ballistic strikes from the south and increased recruitment of foreign fighters. Diplomatic tensions rose with Russia threatening UN legal action over alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states.
key_points: Russian aircraft dropped FAB‑250 bombs on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two and damaging 14 multi‑storey buildings, an education facility and administrative structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
A FAB strike on a multi‑storey building in Druzkovka, Donetsk region, killed a 70‑year‑old pensioner and wounded five adults (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
Ukrainian security services detained a Russian artillery spotter nicknamed “Shaman” in Lyman and eliminated the accompanying combatant (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).

Multiple UAVs were reported over northern oblasts, including a reactive UAV from Bryansk into northern Chernihiv and several UAVs heading toward Kyiv water reservoirs (Source: @eRadarrua/93683, @kpszsu/62786).
Russia warned of possible ballistic weapon use from the south and launched guided aerial bombs in Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62770).
Russian Foreign Ministry announced strikes targeting Ukrainian defense industry facilities and command centres in Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russia signaled intent to file a complaint at the UN International Court of Justice regarding alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in Baltic countries (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).

sections.executive_summary: Russian FAB‑250 strikes on Kramatorsk and Druzkovka caused civilian casualties and extensive building damage. Ukraine reported numerous UAV incursions across northern and central fronts, while Russia announced plans for ballistic attacks from the south and increased foreign fighter recruitment. Diplomatic friction escalated as Russia threatened UN legal action over Baltic‑region ethnic Russian issues.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported that Russian forces dropped five FAB‑250 bombs on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two people and damaging 14 multi‑storey residential buildings, an educational institution, administrative structures and a New Post office (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
In Druzkovka, Donetsk region, a Russian FAB strike destroyed a multi‑storey building, killing a 70‑year‑old pensioner and wounding five civilians aged 40‑73 (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).

The Security Service of Ukraine (SBU) captured a Russian artillery spotter codenamed “Shaman” in Lyman, who had been coordinating fire with a local collaborator before being killed during a special operation (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).

Ukrainian forces continue to hold the front near the village of Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region for over 1,500 days, earning a record entry for the 118th Mechanized Brigade (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).

Russia’s Foreign Ministry announced that its armed forces are beginning consistent strikes against Ukrainian defense‑industry facilities in Kyiv and will target decision‑making centres and command posts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russian officials warned of a potential ballistic weapons threat emanating from the southern direction, indicating preparation for long‑range strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62758).
The Russian Ministry of Defence reported guided aerial bomb deployments over Donetsk region as part of its tactical aviation operations (Source: @kpszsu/62770).
Russia disclosed that it has recruited over 28,000 foreign citizens for its army, with at least 5,000 already killed in the war against Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81455).
Ukrainian reports indicated a reactive UAV crossing from Bryansk into northern Chernihiv oblast, suggesting Russian aerial reconnaissance activity near the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93683).
Multiple UAVs were observed heading toward Kyiv water reservoirs from Chernihiv and other northern oblasts, indicating continued pressure on critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62786).
Ukrainian forces recorded UAV activity over Sumy and Kharkiv directions, with several strike groups moving toward key settlements such as Novomykolayivka and Balakliya (Source: @eRadarrua/93681).
A group of UAVs was reported over Mykolaiv region heading toward Snihurivka and Voskresenske, showing expanded UAV operations toward the southern‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62780).
UAV sightings included a reactive UAV over northern Chernihiv heading from Bryansk, and additional UAVs from Chernihiv moving toward Kyiv water reservoirs (Source: @eRadarrua/93683, @kpszsu/62786).
Reports from the north of Sumy indicated UAVs traveling toward Shostka, while UAVs from northern Zhytomyr were directed to Ovrych (Source: @kpszsu/62787).
A pair of UAVs were observed over Zhytomyr’s Korosten area, one identified as reactive, highlighting ongoing aerial activity in the northern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/93693).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a UAV from Chernihiv heading toward Slavutych, suggesting potential targeting of strategic locations near the capital (Source: @eRadarrua/93697).
Russian ballistic threat from the south was announced, implying possible long‑range missile or artillery use against Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62758).
Guided aerial bomb launches were reported over Donetsk, indicating intensified Russian air operations on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/62770).
UAV groups were observed over Mykolaiv heading toward Snihurivka, a key logistics hub in the southern theater (Source: @kpszsu/62780).
No specific ground engagements were detailed in the source messages for the southern front beyond the mentioned UAV activity.
Two Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were reported relocating from Olenya airfield to the Far East, reflecting Russian strategic bomber redeployment (Source: @eRadarrua/93655).

Multiple UAV incursions across northern and central Ukraine were documented, including reactive UAVs and groups targeting water reservoirs and transport corridors (Source: @eRadarrua/93681, @kpszsu/62787).
Russia announced upcoming strikes on Kyiv’s defense‑industry facilities and command centres, indicating a focus on air‑strike campaigns against critical infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Ukrainian forces reported the downing or neutralization of a Russian UAV spotter in Lyman, showing active air‑defence and counter‑UAV operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).

key_developments: Russian aircraft dropped FAB‑250 bombs on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two and damaging 14 multi‑storey buildings, an education facility and administrative structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
A FAB strike on a multi‑storey building in Druzkovka, Donetsk region, killed a 70‑year‑old pensioner and wounded five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
Ukrainian security services detained a Russian artillery spotter nicknamed “Shaman” in Lyman and eliminated the accompanying combatant (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).

Multiple UAVs were reported over northern oblasts, including a reactive UAV from Bryansk into Chernihiv and several UAVs heading toward Kyiv water reservoirs (Source: @eRadarrua/93683, @kpszsu/62786).
Russia warned of a possible ballistic weapons threat from the south, indicating preparation for long‑range strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62758).
The Russian Foreign Ministry announced strikes targeting Ukrainian defense‑industry facilities and command centres in Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russia signaled intent to file a complaint at the UN International Court of Justice over alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in Baltic states (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial activity remains high, with FAB‑250 bombings causing civilian casualties and extensive infrastructure damage in eastern Ukraine. UAV incursions across northern and central regions suggest continued reconnaissance and strike capabilities, while announced ballistic threats from the south indicate a potential escalation to longer‑range attacks. The recruitment of foreign fighters and diplomatic posturing further complicate the security environment, increasing pressure on Ukrainian defensive operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces have intensified air‑defence measures, successfully neutralizing UAV spotters and reporting increased UAV interceptions. Local authorities are reinforcing critical infrastructure protection, especially water reservoirs near Kyiv. International partners continue to monitor Russian recruitment efforts and diplomatic threats, with potential implications for sanctions and support to Ukraine.
sections.intelligence_notes: The FAB‑250 strikes provide concrete evidence of Russia's willingness to employ heavy munitions against civilian areas, underscoring the need for enhanced civil defence protocols. UAV flight paths from Bryansk into Chernihiv and toward Kyiv highlight gaps in airspace monitoring that require immediate attention. The announced ballistic threat from the south warrants monitoring of missile launch sites and potential early‑warning system upgrades.
